record.field: Apache Answer is an open-source Q&A platform that turns scattered team knowledge into a structured, searchable community.

Apache Answer is an open-source Q&A platform that turns scattered team knowledge into a structured, searchable community.

Developed under the Apache Software Foundation, Answer lets you run your own question-and-answer service for community forums, help centers, or internal knowledge bases. It positions itself as a self-hosted alternative to Stack Overflow for Teams, giving you full ownership of your data with no per-user fees. Built in Go with a React/TypeScript frontend, it ships as a single Docker-friendly application backed by MySQL, PostgreSQL, or SQLite.

Key features
  • Structured questions and answers with tags, voting, and accepted answers
  • Reputation, badges, and gamification to reward quality contributions
  • Full-text search plus a Markdown editor with code syntax highlighting
  • Plugin architecture for OAuth login, S3 storage, external search, and anti-spam
  • Roles, moderation tools, and fine-grained access control
  • 40+ language packs and a RESTful API for integrations
Best for

Teams and communities that need a searchable knowledge base or support forum: engineering orgs documenting internal know-how, product help centers, and open communities seeking a self-hosted alternative to Stack Overflow for Teams.

services: answer: image: apache/answer:1.4.1 ports: - '{{ PORT_HTTP }}:9080' restart: on-failure volumes: - ./answer_data:/data depends_on: db: condition: service_healthy container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}' networks: - internal - agz-proxy-network db: image: postgres:16 restart: always healthcheck: test: - CMD-SHELL - pg_isready -U $${POSTGRES_USER} -d $${POSTGRES_DB} interval: 5s timeout: 5s retries: 5 volumes: - ./db_data:/var/lib/postgresql/data environment: POSTGRES_DB: answer POSTGRES_USER: postgres POSTGRES_PASSWORD: postgres container_name: '{{ INSTANCE_CODE }}_db' networks: - internal networks: internal: driver: bridge agz-proxy-network: external: true

#!/bin/bash set -euo pipefail cd "$SERVICE_PATH" # Dump consistente de PostgreSQL (servicio en marcha) → $BACKUP_DATA_DIR docker compose exec -T db sh -c 'pg_dump -U "$POSTGRES_USER" -d "$POSTGRES_DB" --no-owner -Fc' \ > "$BACKUP_DATA_DIR/db.dump" echo "pg_dump OK ($(wc -c < "$BACKUP_DATA_DIR/db.dump") bytes)"

#!/bin/bash set -euo pipefail cd "$SERVICE_PATH" # Levanta solo la DB, espera readiness y restaura docker compose up -d db for i in $(seq 1 30); do docker compose exec -T db sh -c 'pg_isready -U "$POSTGRES_USER"' && break sleep 2 done docker compose exec -T db sh -c 'pg_restore -U "$POSTGRES_USER" -d "$POSTGRES_DB" --clean --if-exists --no-owner' \ < "$RESTORE_DATA_DIR/db.dump" # Verificación: la DB no quedó vacía (sh -c con dobles comillas; $VAR del contenedor con \$) N=$(docker compose exec -T db sh -c "psql -U \$POSTGRES_USER -d \$POSTGRES_DB -tAc \"SELECT count(*) FROM information_schema.tables WHERE table_schema='public'\"") echo "tablas restauradas: $N" test "$N" -gt 0
